title: Custom malloc()
tagline: Memory allocator built from scratch in C
description: A from-scratch implementation of malloc, free, and realloc in C. Uses a best-fit allocation strategy with a doubly linked free list, block coalescing to prevent heap fragmentation, and a global heap managed with sbrk(). No libc — just raw memory management the way the OS sees it.
challenges: The hardest part was getting coalescing right — merging adjacent free blocks on every free() call without corrupting the linked list. Required careful pointer arithmetic and extensive testing with edge cases like zero-size allocations and re-freeing.
results: A working allocator that handles allocation, freeing, and reuse without leaks — built entirely without standard library memory functions.

title: Eduvedh
description: Frontend for a startup's educational news and blog platform, built as a freelance engagement. Implemented a reader-facing React site and a separate admin panel, both in TypeScript/Vite. Features include a Jodit rich-text editor, anonymous commenting with IP tracking, AI-generated article summaries via Claude Haiku, Cloudinary image handling, and newsletter delivery through Resend.
challenges: Managing two separate Vite apps (reader and admin) with shared auth state and role-based access across SUPER_ADMIN, ADMIN, and EDITOR roles. Also handled a mid-project database migration from Supabase to Railway PostgreSQL using pg_dump with custom restore flags.
results: Delivered a production freelance project with AI summaries, newsletter integration, and RBAC — shipped and handed off to the client.

title: ASKpro IoT Dashboard
tagline: Centralized device management platform for an IoT company
description: Internship project built with two teammates for ASKpro, an IoT company. A web dashboard for managing three device types — Water ATM, Telemetry units, and Batch Controllers. Backend in Node.js/Express/TypeScript with Prisma 7, Zod validation, and PostgreSQL on Railway. Frontend in Next.js. Implements RBAC, cookie-based JWT auth, and a pull-based polling pattern so devices fetch their own config updates rather than receiving pushes.
challenges: Designing a config-sync pattern that works reliably for IoT devices with intermittent connectivity — settled on pull-based polling so devices request their own state rather than relying on persistent connections that could drop.
results: Functional internal dashboard used by ASKpro to manage their deployed device fleet, with role-based access for different operator levels.

title: Hotel & Bar POS
tagline: Offline-first desktop POS system for Maharashtra hospitality
description: A private desktop application for hotel and bar point-of-sale operations. Built with Electron as the shell, a pkg-compiled Node/TypeScript backend bundled inside the app, a React frontend, and a portable PostgreSQL 16 instance — so the entire stack runs locally with no internet dependency. Handles orders, billing, and inventory without a cloud dependency.
challenges: Bundling a compiled backend binary, React frontend, and portable PostgreSQL into a single distributable Electron app. Getting cross-origin auth working correctly in the file-origin Electron context required careful CORS and cookie config.
results: A self-contained desktop POS that installs and runs without any external dependencies — database included.
